cpSummary: The 2052 buyers across the eight UK Electric zapper ASINs (Buzbug, YISSVIC × 2, GeckoMan, Fly-Bye, LiBa, mafiti, Jawlark) are mostly homeowners and garden / patio users looking for a passive, chemical-free way to control flies, mosquitoes and the occasional wasp. A meaningful sub-segment are parents and pet owners who explicitly reject chemical sprays, plus a smaller commercial group (pub, cafe and shop owners) who buy multiple units for food-service environments.

usageScenarios.label: Summer-peak wasp invasion response
usageScenarios.reason: Bought reactively in July/August when wasps invade — installed at the back door or patio.

usageScenarios.label: Indoor fly + wasp control in the kitchen / conservatory
usageScenarios.reason: Plug-in unit on a worktop or window ledge — clears flies during the day and the odd indoor wasp.

usageScenarios.label: Bedroom mosquito / midge zapper at night
usageScenarios.reason: Quiet, low-glow models used in the bedroom to kill mosquitoes and midges before sleep.

usageScenarios.label: Patio / decking BBQ protection
usageScenarios.reason: Portable or wall-mounted outdoor units around the BBQ and dining area in summer.

usageScenarios.label: Garage / shed insect control
usageScenarios.reason: Workshop and storage areas where flies congregate — buyers want a low-maintenance always-on unit.

usageScenarios.label: Pub / cafe / shop fly control
usageScenarios.reason: Commercial-grade UV units mounted high on a wall, on all day during food service hours.

csSummary: Sentiment is split: 45.1% positive (4–5★) and 54.9% negative (1–3★). Successful buyers — typically indoor users in conservatories, kitchens and pub gardens — report trays full of dead insects and recommend the units enthusiastically. Negative reviews concentrate on three structural issues: poor wasp efficacy (the unit catches flies but not wasps), noise and bright light at night (incompatible with bedroom use), and outdoor durability failures (rain ingress, rust). Bee/moth kills draw smaller but vocal ecological backlash that disproportionately damages star ratings.

negativeTopics.label: Doesn't catch wasps / ineffective
negativeTopics.reason: Buyers report the zapper kills very few wasps (often only flies/midges) — the single biggest source of frustration.
bullets: 268 buyers (23.8% of negative reviews) say the zapper either catches nothing or only catches small flies/midges, never the wasps the unit was bought for.
A common pattern: indoor models work on flies but wasps stay outside in daylight; the UV light only competes after dark when wasps are dormant.
Buyers compare the unit to homemade jam-jar wasp traps and conclude the DIY option catches more wasps for far less money.
When wasps are abundant outdoors (BBQs, pub gardens), reviewers feel the zapper makes no visible dent in the population.
quotes: "I've purchased this bug zapper in May 2022."
"Updated review: This item was taken out of the sunroom after my review in April, recharged, and put in the kitchen in case of flies."
"Having dogs and living in the country isn’t always a pleasant experience, I like to leave the door to garden open so that they can come and go as they please."
"Bugs were not attracted to this device at my house."

negativeTopics.label: Flimsy / hard-to-clean tray
negativeTopics.reason: Cleaning the catch tray is fiddly and dead insects stick to the grid; many units feel cheaply made and break early.
bullets: 18.7% of negative reviews flag a flimsy build or fiddly tray — dead insects stick to the grid and don't come out.
Plastic catch trays crack, hinges break, and the gap between grid and tray traps debris that's hard to brush out.
Cheap plug or worn cable is a recurring failure point on lower-priced units.
quotes: "I really wanted this to work, but unfortunately, the LiBa Electric Bug Zapper was a complete waste of money."
"The result is that you kill nothing unless the fly happens to get stuck in the mesh."
"I purchased LIBA zapper on 3/22/24 and both tubes went out within the three and four months 6/19/24 and 7/16/24."
"I bought product December 11 2023 and I must say it works pretty well."

negativeTopics.label: Loud zap / buzzing noise
negativeTopics.reason: The crackle of each kill plus a constant hum disturbs sleep and conversation, especially indoors at night.
bullets: 47 buyers describe the constant hum or the loud zap-crackle as unbearable indoors at night.
When a wasp or larger fly hits the grid, the bang can be loud enough to wake light sleepers.
Buyers in flats and conservatories say neighbours and pets react badly to the noise.
quotes: "Having dogs and living in the country isn’t always a pleasant experience, I like to leave the door to garden open so that they can come and go as they please."
"Bought this during the heat wave in July as I had noticed quite a few flies in the house with the windows being open."
"I've had a couple of these swatters and are a good way to quickly deal with fruit flies, until it stops working."
"We ordered this when we were overwhelmed with fruit flies we accidentally brought home in a farmer's market haul."

negativeTopics.label: Safety concerns (grid, shocks, kids/pets)
negativeTopics.reason: Exposed grids and the risk of shocks worry parents and pet owners — buyers want a properly caged design.
bullets: Exposed grid raises shock concerns — parents with toddlers and pet owners want a proper outer cage.
A few reviewers report a burning smell or sparks the first time the unit kills a large insect.
Reviewers who have small children call out the lack of a safety cut-out or child lock.
quotes: "Updated review: This item was taken out of the sunroom after my review in April, recharged, and put in the kitchen in case of flies."
"Having dogs and living in the country isn’t always a pleasant experience, I like to leave the door to garden open so that they can come and go as they please."
"Bought this during the heat wave in July as I had noticed quite a few flies in the house with the windows being open."
"Listen to people when they say that this is a dangerous product."

negativeTopics.label: Arrived broken or DOA
negativeTopics.reason: Glass tubes and plastic housings often arrive cracked or fail within days of first use.
bullets: UV tubes arrive cracked — Amazon's box doesn't protect the unit from carrier handling.
Several reviewers report DOA: plug in, no light, no fan, no zap — refund demanded.
Missing parts (tray, mounting bracket, instructions) drive bad first impressions before the product is even tested.
quotes: "Item arrived faulty, didnt work unless it was plugged in which makes a fly swatter next to useless!"
"Opened used item sent to me, was also missing the power cable and has a broken piece on the racket."
"Would not turn on out of the box, just a small flicker of blue light for a second"
"only opened box after return deadline"

negativeTopics.label: Too bright in the bedroom
negativeTopics.reason: UV bulbs light up bedrooms and stop people sleeping — a common complaint when used at night indoors.
bullets: 0.4% of negative reviews call the UV bulb too bright for bedroom use.
Buyers expecting "subtle blue glow" find a strong purple light that lights up the entire room.
Several reviewers move the unit to the landing or bathroom because they cannot sleep with it on.

negativeTopics.label: Not weatherproof for outdoor use
negativeTopics.reason: Models marketed as outdoor fail after rain — water gets in, electrics short, units rust.
bullets: Outdoor models stop working after the first heavy rain — water gets into the housing and the electrics short.
Steel grids rust within weeks if left in the garden — buyers feel the IP rating is overstated.
UK weather (constant damp) is hostile to even "weatherproof" units; some buyers bring them in every night.
quotes: "If there is even a tiniest opening in your home for even a miniscule insect to enter indoor, then indoor flies will only continue to thrive, if not get worse."
"it died after a 10 minute rain, which during the summer, we will have many."

negativeTopics.label: Kills bees and other beneficial insects
negativeTopics.reason: UV light is non-selective — bees, moths and ladybirds end up in the tray, drawing strong ecological backlash.
bullets: 0.1% of negative reviews specifically call out bee, moth or butterfly kills as ecologically unacceptable.
Beekeepers and gardeners are the most vocal critics — some give 1 stars purely on the basis of bee mortality.
Several reviewers note that "everything is in the tray except wasps" — the unit is non-selective.
Even buyers who like the kill-count are uncomfortable when they see ladybirds and moths among the dead.

negativeInsights.finding: UV zappers catch flies and midges well but largely fail against wasps — buyers expecting wasp-specific results feel mis-sold.
negativeInsights.implication: Re-position UK Electric SKUs as "fly + mosquito" first, "wasp" only as a secondary claim. Pair with a complementary lure-trap accessory for wasps to capture both segments without over-promising.

negativeInsights.finding: Beekeeper and naturalist backlash against bee/moth kills shows up in 1-star reviews and damages the listing's credibility.
negativeInsights.implication: A+ content must address selectivity head-on — promote indoor-only use cases and consider a bee-safe outdoor variant with selective baiting (or skip outdoor positioning entirely).

negativeInsights.finding: Loud zap noise and bright UV light make most units unsuitable for the bedroom — buyers explicitly move the unit out at night.
negativeInsights.implication: Launch a "Bedroom" SKU: vacuum-fan capture (silent), warm-amber UV-A bulb (less glare), low-power night mode. Premium pricing justified by sleep-friendly engineering.

negativeInsights.finding: UK buyers in 2024–2026 explicitly factor electricity cost into reviews — running a 9–20W bulb 24/7 is now a barrier.
negativeInsights.implication: Energy-saving messaging on the listing ("only 9W — pennies a night"); consider a motion/dusk sensor variant that switches off when no insects detected.

negativeInsights.finding: Cheap plastic trays, brittle UV tubes and DOA units cluster in the lowest-priced YISSVIC / generic listings — drags average rating below 4.0.
negativeInsights.implication: Premiumise the build (stronger tray, shock-mounted tube) and add a 2-year warranty badge; let cheap competitors keep the bottom of the price band.

negativeInsights.finding: Models marketed as "outdoor" fail in UK rain — water ingress shorts the electrics and rusts the grid within weeks.
negativeInsights.implication: Either invest in a genuine IP65+ housing and certify it, or remove "outdoor" from the title and main image. False outdoor claims are the single biggest source of returns in this category.

positiveTopics.label: Loads of dead flies / wasps every morning
positiveTopics.reason: Successful buyers report trays full of dead insects within days — the visible kill count is the strongest satisfaction driver.
bullets: 282 buyers report a tray full of dead flies, midges or wasps within the first week.
Conservatory and kitchen owners are the most vocal positive segment — confined spaces let the UV do its job.
Buyers describe being "amazed" by the kill count and recommend installing one per main living area.
Repeat purchasers buy a second unit for upstairs after the first works downstairs.
quotes: "Holy hell, when I tell you the summer of 2024 was hell for my UK home, it was."
"The Mafiti Fly Zapper is a powerful and efficient device designed to eliminate pesky flies from your indoor spaces."

"We used to have a basic fly zapper in the house that used batteries but part of it broke very early on and it was held together with tape, so I always refused to it as I didn’t fancy getting zapped."
"I like the slim width which allows for placement options I didn't have with another zapper."

positiveTopics.label: Great value — pays for itself
positiveTopics.reason: Buyers compare to spray and pest-control callouts and conclude the zapper is cheap insurance for the summer.
bullets: Buyers compare to a £40+ pest-control callout and conclude a £20–30 zapper is "cheap insurance" for the summer.
Bundle reviews (2-pack) report best value — one unit per floor of the house.
Reusable bulb makes the per-summer cost low; replacement tubes are seen as a cheap consumable.

I have been using this for four months, with it on constantly."

"Considering that I had almost mastered using the standard swatter, this swatter has made my life a breeze with the mesh  that can be angled to suit."

positiveTopics.label: Durable — second / third summer in use
positiveTopics.reason: Repeat-summer reviews are a strong trust signal — proves the unit survives a UK winter in storage.
bullets: Repeat-summer reviews ("third summer and still going strong") are the strongest durability signal.
Buyers note the unit survives being stored in a damp shed over winter — robust enough for UK conditions.
quotes: "Holy hell, when I tell you the summer of 2024 was hell for my UK home, it was."

"We used to have a basic fly zapper in the house that used batteries but part of it broke very early on and it was held together with tape, so I always refused to it as I didn’t fancy getting zapped."

"Now that the warm weather is here we are already being invaded by flies who can get into the house through the smallest of gaps but seem incapable of finding the huge exit via an open door and window!"
"I was astonished when I unpacked these fly zappers."

positiveTopics.label: Easy to empty — slide-out tray
positiveTopics.reason: Removable tray that tips straight into the bin is the most-praised design feature.
bullets: 25 buyers single out the slide-out tray as the best design feature — empty, wipe, slide back.
Removable mesh grid (washable under the tap) is a strong purchase trigger over fixed-grid models.
Buyers compare to chemical fly papers: the zapper is cleaner, no sticky mess.
quotes: "The Mafiti Fly Zapper is a powerful and efficient device designed to eliminate pesky flies from your indoor spaces."
"I like the slim width which allows for placement options I didn't have with another zapper."

"Updated Review (After 4 months of use)

I have been using this for four months, with it on constantly."
"Fly Zapper is an indispensable tool for maintaining a fly-free environment in your home, kitchen, indoor garden, or yard."

positiveTopics.label: Works indoors and out — patio, kitchen, bedroom
positiveTopics.reason: Versatile placement (kitchen counter by day, bedroom or patio by night) drives heavy daily use.
bullets: Versatile placement is a strong purchase driver — same unit on the kitchen counter by day and the patio table at night.
Portable / rechargeable models extend usage to the garden table and BBQ area where mains units cannot reach.
quotes: "Summertime always means we get a lot of flies in the house."
"I've had this product for over 2 months now and I'm still loving it."
"The Mafiti Fly Zapper is a godsend for anyone plagued by pesky insects during the warmer months."
"It comes with 2 spare light bulbs."

positiveTopics.label: Modern / discreet design — looks like a lamp
positiveTopics.reason: Lantern-style and matte-black models are praised for blending into living rooms and patios — far better than the old neon-tube look.
bullets: Lantern-style and matte-black models are praised for not looking like a "1980s pub zapper".
Buyers leave the unit visible on the kitchen counter or bedside table because the design is acceptable.
Compact size (small enough for a worktop) is a key reason buyers chose the unit over larger competitors.
quotes: "The Mafiti Fly Zapper is a powerful and efficient device designed to eliminate pesky flies from your indoor spaces."
"I like the slim width which allows for placement options I didn't have with another zapper."

"I have a couple of insect zappers one is excellent but very small and does not have a protective mesh so it was zap anything of any size whilst the other has a protective mesh which makes it useless on larger insects."
"Fly Zapper is an indispensable tool for maintaining a fly-free environment in your home, kitchen, indoor garden, or yard."

positiveInsights.finding: Buyers in conservatories, kitchens and pub gardens report tray-full kill counts within days — visible results are the strongest satisfaction trigger.
positiveInsights.implication: Lead the main image with a "tray full of insects after 1 night" social-proof shot. Use Buzbug-style results photography in A+ content to convert browsers.

positiveInsights.finding: Quiet operation is the single biggest differentiator vs old-style crackle zappers — vacuum-fan models score noticeably higher.
positiveInsights.implication: Decibel-rating spec on the listing ("<25 dB — quieter than a fridge"). Vacuum-capture sub-brand for the bedroom segment.

positiveInsights.finding: Parents and pet owners actively choose electric over chemical sprays — "no chemicals, no poison" is a cited purchase reason.
positiveInsights.implication: "Chemical-free" badge in the main image; pair with a child-safe caged-grid variant aimed explicitly at family households.

positiveInsights.finding: Lantern-style and matte-black aesthetics are praised for "looking like a lamp, not a zapper" — buyers leave units on display.
positiveInsights.implication: Lifestyle photography (bedside table, kitchen counter, patio table) showing the unit in a styled room. Premiumise via design, not just specs.

positiveInsights.finding: Repeat buyers want a second unit for the bedroom or upstairs after the first works downstairs.
positiveInsights.implication: 2-pack and 3-pack bundles ("Whole-home protection") at 15–20% saving. Subscription replacement bulbs for repeat AOV.

positiveInsights.finding: Buyers benchmark against £40+ pest-control callouts and conclude a £20–30 zapper is cheap insurance.
positiveInsights.implication: "Cheaper than one pest-control visit" comparison in A+ content. Emphasise reusability (years of summers from one unit) to justify mid-tier pricing.

buyersMotivation.label: Tired of swatting flies and wasps every summer
buyersMotivation.reason: Default trigger: warm weather brings flies into the kitchen and wasps to the patio — buyers want a passive solution.

buyersMotivation.label: Avoid chemicals around children and pets
buyersMotivation.reason: Parents and pet owners reject sprays and poisons; an electric unit feels like a clean, family-safe alternative.

buyersMotivation.label: Sleep without mosquitoes / midges
buyersMotivation.reason: Bedroom-focused buyers want overnight protection — this is the strongest pull for quiet, low-glow units.

buyersMotivation.label: BBQ / patio season — protect outdoor dining
buyersMotivation.reason: Summer entertaining drives portable and outdoor-rated SKUs — buyers want flies away from food and drink.

buyersMotivation.label: Conservatory / kitchen fly problem all year
buyersMotivation.reason: Year-round indoor use case — flies bred from compost or food waste need constant suppression.

buyersMotivation.label: Cheaper than calling pest control
buyersMotivation.reason: Buyers explicitly compare £20–30 zapper to a £40+ professional callout and pick the DIY route.

customerExpectations.label: Genuinely silent operation for the bedroom
customerExpectations.reason: Buyers expect a "noise-free" claim to mean truly inaudible at 2m — current units crackle and hum.

customerExpectations.label: Selective wasp / fly attraction (not bees or moths)
customerExpectations.reason: Strong expectation that the unit will not kill beneficial insects — non-selectivity is a deal-breaker for ecological buyers.

customerExpectations.label: Easy-empty tray with no hand contact
customerExpectations.reason: Slide-out tray that tips straight into the bin — minimal cleaning, no touching dead insects.

customerExpectations.label: Genuine IP65 outdoor durability
customerExpectations.reason: Outdoor SKUs must survive UK rain and damp — current "outdoor" claims fail within weeks.

customerExpectations.label: Low energy consumption (visible spec)
customerExpectations.reason: Buyers want the wattage on the listing and want it under 10W — explicitly to keep the electricity bill down.

customerExpectations.label: Clear placement / setup instructions
customerExpectations.reason: How high, how far from food, indoor vs outdoor — buyers want a visual placement card to set expectations on results.

reviews.t:
Total Disappointment – Doesn’t Work as Advertised — I really wanted this to work, but unfortunately, the LiBa Electric Bug Zapper was a complete waste of money. I bought it specifically to deal with mosquitoes and flies in my home, but it simply doesn’t do the job. I followed all instructions, plugged it in, and left it running for several nights — not a single mosquito or fly was zapped.

The light does turn on and the grid looks like it should be powerful, but it’s just not attracting anything. I even placed it in darker areas where bugs usually gather — still nothing. Meanwhile, I’m gettin…

Massive fire hazard, do not buy! — Listen to people when they say that this is a dangerous product. It's a fire waiting to happen. I used it for about a year and this was amazing for reducing the fly population in our house. However, one day, I woke up to it constantly zapping. The whole house smelt like burnt plastic. It was the end of the season, so I unplugged it and went back to sleep.
Warm weather comes again, and I plug it back in, and it's back to the constant zapping. I thought maybe there's a fly carcass still stuck in there but nope, couldn't find anything.
So I unplugged the device…

Ineffective and potentially dangerous — I bought this as a large replacement fro the previous smaller fly catcher and am genuinely disappointed. The catch rate does not seem to be as effective. Indeed, I found one fruit fly sitting happily on the bulb, having obviously succeeded in flying through the grid. I also have serious concerns regarding safety. The cleaning process requires one to slide the bottom tray past the charged grid, for which the instructions state: "Cut off the power and connect the two wires in the defensive net with the screwdriver to discharge". On the one hand this impli…

Faulty button. DANGEROUS!!! — I've had a couple of these swatters and are a good way to quickly deal with fruit flies, until it stops working. Maybe a design flaw but the button you need to hold down is garbage, worked for 2 days, now it's useless. The swatter power comes on and off. Impossible to keep electrified long enough to deal with insects. Avoid this item, going by some reviews, seems the seller ignores purchasers that want to return item. I won't waste any more of my time by returning it. In my opinion, the cheaper ones are better designed than this.

Edit. This device has now starte…

Zapper faulty and uv tubes damaged. — One of the two uv tubes was not working. The two safe tubes supplied were damaged. I purchased two replacement tubes of the correct specification. Neither of these would work when fitted in the sockets replacing the tube that was not working when the zapper was delivered. Can only assume there is a fault with the product. Contacted the seller twice and no offer of a replacement.
Have thrown the zapper away and purchased an alternative brand which works exceptionally well. A bonus is that there is no loud crack like a rifle shot when a fly touches the elec…

This product fails at a very high rate. — This product is the ultimate example of poor Chinese quality products.  When they work, they do work well.  However over the last several years we have bought several of them.  Because they keep dying.  Not the bulbs themselves.  Yes they'll burnout fast.  But the actual light units themselves.  I've seen a couple that actually melted around where the bulb is attached.  And 1 that just stopped working altogether.  Personally I would consider this a fire hazard waiting.  I would strongly recommend to anyone I knew to never buy this product.  Which is t…

Inhumane, cruel, useless! — This product is so weak that one zap only maimed the fly, a small fruit fly not anything huge. After one zap it was flopping around on the floor half dead. I thought I would rotate the racket flat to the floor and put it out of its misery but the mesh is not flush to the edge of the plastic surround so it doesn't actually touch the floor or the fly sat on it! I ended up having to get some kitchen roll out to do the job myself 🤢 Also the light function is pointless, it attracts nothing. Returned and bought a less fancy, battery operated one that actually works for a…

Doesn’t work very well — Disappointed. It looks well made. But it doesn’t work well enough. It’s not as good as The executioner. When you swat a fly you need to really get some energy behind the swipe, otherwise the fly just walks on the outside of the metalwork where the electric current isn’t there. You need to have enough pressure in your swing to make the fly go inside the metalwork.
The automatic mode doesn’t do anything. The power is on. The light is on. But it doesn’t attract flies even in a dark room. Presumably because the light is so dim.
I’d like to return but don’t have original p…

A real Fly Catcher! Bulbs are not Lasting many weeks — very effective Machine...keeps my Kitchen/Diner free of Flies. Catches Moths too in the evening. The only thing against it in my opinion is the loud snapping noise when it Zaps a fly. Really makes you jump at times.
The Bulbs don't last very long . One only 4weeks. The original appliance I bought lasted around a year. I bought a replacement Machine and returned as it did not work. Reordered new one only worked on one Bulb. Tried changing the Bulb still didn't work so being returned. I will now order more Bulbs for the original one and hop…

Bulbs socket stop working — Bought this in 2023, used it for about 8 hours during the night and turned it off during the day. The bulb sockets stop working after a few days, but I thought it was just the bulbs. After buying replacement bulbs, I noticed that only the upper light was turning on, but not the lower ones even after swapping new bulbs. I didn't care too much as only one bulb was needed for it to work, but after a few days of usage both bulb sockets stopped working. The electric grid is still working, but without the bulbs working it won't be as effective.

Fire hazard — Before you buy this product please bear in mind it’s a fire hazard. I’m glad the two occasions it happened I was at home if not it would’ve been a different story entirely. I bought 2 and after a few months it I was hearing spark noise and when I checked there was smoke everywhere, one in the bug zapper was burning. After a month the second one also did the same thing. I’m glad I took a video of the second event. I’m seeing other reviews of same complain, the company needs to look into whatever is causing the  fire because it’s terrible.

Works well whilst it works but also seems dangerous — Works well but ended up burning through bulbs fast and died after 6 months

Be aware replacement bulbs are about half the cost of the machine if you can find good deals and you will definitely need them as they burn through after a few months, replacing the bulbs seems very dangerous as it’s clearly not earthed well, the instructions recommend turning off and unplugging then hitting the metal contacts with a screwdriver until it sparks!

It also seemed to only last long enough to outlive the return window. It is cheaply made.

This is a very well functioning idea BUT NOT FROM THIS BRAND! — Do not purchase this product; it stopped working after just four weeks. We had previously purchased another brand that we were extremely satisfied with, so we decided to buy four more to have in each room. This brand included an additional piece to make it longer, so we purchased this brand. However, we’ve only used it sparingly since October 5, and today, November 12, as you can see in the attached picture, it no longer works. The light and electric indicator turn on, but there is no power running through the wires.

Doesn’t kill large flies — Plugged this in around 5.00pm and there’s been a regular ‘crack’ every 10-15 minutes ever since.

However, after hearing a ‘crack’ I’d check and see a fly in the tray, but if I stayed to watch I’d see the fly start moving again after a few seconds, and a few minutes later it’s disappeared out of the tray altogether.

It’s now 9.00pm and there’s just two tiny flies in the tray.

I’ll be sending this back in a couple of days and will buy fly paper which deals with every fly that lands on it, at a fraction of the cost.

It work, but until it didn’t FIRE HAZARD!!!!! — My daughter came and told us that a fly or a bug got caught in the bug zapper and it kept popping so we all rushed into the living room to find that it was popping because it was malfunctioning, and it was smoking. We immediately unplugged it. It was smoking very bad it was a smell of electrical burning Praise God our house didn’t  burned down. Do not buy this! Do not go to sleep while this is on let alone plugged in, I will not be buying any more after this has happened. Proceeded with caution.

Does not work effectively enough — when I bought this, I joked with my son that it only puts the flies to sleep as I didnt want to upset him.. Sadly this is all it actually does. I cant speak in regards to mosquitos but this zapper does not work for flies/blowflies. even with the impressive zap sound all that happens is the bugs are knocked unconscious but soon get back to normal. and most not even that. one blowfly wanted to prove its ineffectiveness so much that it actually walked over the wires and then flew off.

Almost started a fire — It was working fine for over 1.5 years until last week when the lights went out and there was constant zapping sound every second. I look over at it and saw it sparking constantly. As I ran over to turn it off, I could smell the burnt plastic smell. Up close, the sparking looked really dangerous so I unplugged it from the wall instead. It’s a major fire hazard! Almost started a fire.  Luckily I was nearby and it was during the day when we’re awake.

Useless, flies ignored it — In this recent hot weather, there has been a proliferation of houseflies buzzing in and out. So much so I have to keep the windows and doors closed.

I bought this as a solution however it quickly became clear that it doesn't work for my purposes. Plugged it in, opened the door and flies began flitting in and out. NONE of them seemed attracted to the machine.

After two hours and at least a dozen flies, I conclude this as a failed experiment.

Doesn't Work — Doesn't work. We have a clean house but have been hit with a fly infestation due to nearby livestock in fields. This has been plugged in for 24 hours and not zapped one fly. There were about 30 flies in the small utility room where they appear to congregate. They're having a jolly time flying around it. I don't know how it has so many amazing reviews. Such a shame, we really wanted this to work. Will be returning for a refund.

Does not work…defective? — It does not work. It’s just a big bright light, but does nothing. I put it in my room, turn off the light and lock the door. When I came back 45 mins later, the mosquitoes were still gallivanting in my room. Not one mosquito was killed.
I decided to put it outside, and nothing, not even a one pop. The light just brightens the veranda, but does nothing. Based on other reviews, I must hand received a defective product.

Burned out, lots of smoke, loud noises — I would be careful with this product. The bulb burned up, a lot of smoke, almost burned my house down, started making loud noises, the video is after I unplugged the first time after it was smoking and cracking like crazy. I plugged it back in and started making these noises again and started smoking again. Immediately unplugged because it was catching on fire. Do not order.  An burn your house down! Waste of money!

Does not work well — Does not work effectively. Love the thought of being able to pivot head and place up on ceiling but thick frame keeps wires too far from fly. Same problem when head is not pivoted. It is clumsy and the thick frame lets too many flies escape.

Returning this product.

Will keep searching for one that works as well as the one we bought two years ago. Sadly it wasn't an Amazon purchase so I can't find it to re-buy.

Packed up in four months — Packed up in only four months and it would seem that there is no way of contacting the company to ask about any  guarantee which I may be entitled to. One lamp works and the lower one does not. I bought two replacement bulbs for this at the same time as purchasing this item - the ones that were recommended for it.
